title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
summary: Ryo Lu discusses how AI changes software development workflows and what aspects of craft must be retained. Topics include the black box risk of AI abstractions, the importance of staying close to material reality rather than abstracting away from it, and the role of tools like Cursor in preserving connection to underlying systems.
writeup: Ryo Lu argues that AI changes software development workflows while certain elements of craft must be retained. The talk frames a black box risk: AI abstractions can hide the underlying systems they generate, pulling developers away from material reality rather than keeping them close to it. The takeaway is that tools like Cursor should preserve connection to underlying systems instead of abstracting them away. The session matters because it addresses what is kept and what is lost as code generation absorbs more of the engineering process.
